title: Manager,Operations
description:
Core Accountabilities / Responsibilities:

  • To develops and implements Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) and guidelines
  • Responsible for overall manpower planning, deployment, scheduling, coordination & supervision of Dept.
  • To prepares the overall manpower loading and reporting to Management
  • Ensures the execution of Project Execution Plan (PEP), including project schedule and budgetary control
  • To oversees budgeting, reporting, planning, and auditing
  • Liaises with other Departments for smooth operation
  • Manages the Operation Department, including the overseeing of all day-to-day project activities
  • Helps to promote company culture that encourages top performance and high morale
  • Identifies and addresses risks and opportunities for the company
  • Develops risk management contingency plan in the operation of the Department
  • Organizes Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) Committee and leads as team leader
  • Conducts monthly safety meeting with OHS Committee
  • Enforces OHS requirements of staffs and workers at workplace
  • Supports worker communication with the Management team
  • Ensures proper documentation are maintained in the Department
  • Responsible for identifying and recording workers’ skills competency
  • Recommends to Management for workforce training and courses according to operational needs
  • Approves leave application for the operation team
  • Manages the OPS Dept. request for supply labour and coordinates with HR according to project needs
  • Conducts ship inspection as required
  • Initiates post mortem meeting review after project completion

Requirements & Qualifications:

Education & Certifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Business Management, Operations Management, or a related field
  • Certifications in Project Management (e.g., PMP,) – advantageous
  • Occupational Health & Safety Certification (e.g. OSHA) – advantageous

Experience:

  • Any relevant Maritime or Logistics certification (if applicable)
  • Preferred minimum 5–10 years of experience in operations, project management, or manpower planning
  • Proven track record in managing teams, workforce planning, and operational execution
  • Experience in budgeting, auditing, and compliance
  • Prior experience in the marine, shipyard, or construction industry (if applicable)

Skills & Competencies:

  • Strong leadership & team management – ability to lead and motivate teams
  • Excellent organizational & planning skills – managing schedules, manpower, and resources
  • Project management & problem-solving – handling risks, opportunities, and post-project reviews
  • Knowledge of OHS regulations – ensuring compliance with safety standards
  • Good communication & stakeholder management – liaising with other departments and workers
  • Proficiency in MS Office & ERP systems – for documentation and reporting
